Reaching for a barnacle-crusted glass bottle half-buried in sand, examining the faded label with wide-eyed curiosity and tentative fingertips.
Pulling herself onto sun-warmed rocks with both hands, powerful tail still trailing in the churning surf below, scales glistening.
Watching a distant sailing ship through the sea spray, one hand shading her eyes, yearning written across her upturned face.
Tracing spiraling patterns in wet sand with extended fingertips as incoming waves wash over her wrists, erasing each design.
Collecting smoothed sea glass and pottery shards along the tide line, cupping the colorful fragments carefully in both palms.
Half-submerged in a rocky tidepool, chin resting on folded arms at the stone edge, watching tiny crabs scuttle past.
Catching falling raindrops in her upturned palms as dark storm clouds roll in low over the churning ocean behind her.
Braiding strands of kelp with small wildflowers plucked from the clifftop grass just within her reach above the waterline.
Pushing off from a weathered dock piling with both hands, glancing back over her shoulder as she slips reluctantly deeper.
Surfacing just enough to peer over a sea-slicked rock at the coastal footpath beyond, only eyes and hair visible.
Lifting a rusted ship's lantern from the shallows, turning it slowly to watch seawater pour from its broken glass panes.
Draping herself across tide-smoothed driftwood, reaching down to draw spirals in the sand pooling beneath the bleached wood.
Untangling a length of frayed rope from around her shoulders, studying the complex knots with furrowed brow and careful fingers.
Pressing a large conch shell against her ear, eyes closed, listening as waves crash rhythmically on the rocks around her.
Balancing a barnacle-encrusted ship's bell on her lap, running curious fingers along its corroded surface and weathered inscriptions.
Leaning forward from shallow water to smell a cluster of beach roses growing wild at the high tide mark.
Holding up a waterlogged book, pages dripping, attempting to decipher the smudged text with her head tilted in concentration.
Wringing saltwater from her long hair with both hands while perched on a rock, rivulets streaming down her bare shoulders.
Examining a child's lost beach bucket, turning the bright plastic over and peering inside at the trapped hermit crab.
Stretching upward to touch wind chimes hanging from a pier beam, making them sing softly in the salt-tinged breeze.
Cradling a cracked ship's compass in her palms, tapping the spinning needle with one fingertip, mesmerized by its movement.
Peeling a waterlogged map open carefully against the rocks, tracing coastlines with one wet finger, brow furrowed in study.
Reaching up to grab the lowest rung of a rusted ladder leading to a pier, testing its weight before releasing.
Sifting through a tangle of fishing net washed ashore, extracting trapped shells and returning them carefully to the water.
Holding a tarnished hand mirror up to catch her reflection, startled by her own face framed in the corroded silver.
